title: Employee Standards of Conduct
description: Conroe ISD holds all employees to clear standards of professional and ethical behavior while they are working with students and representing the district. The policy outlines expectations for how staff should conduct themselves, including rules around honesty, appropriate relationships with students, and responsible use of district resources. Parents and community members can use this policy to understand the behavior the district expects from everyone on its payroll.

timeline_local.summary: The board adopted revisions to strengthen the tobacco/e-cigarette prohibition for employees. The amendment expands the prohibition to explicitly cover e-cigarettes and 'similar products' (aligning with Health and Safety Code §161.081) on District property and while acting within the scope of employment on or off school property, or at school-related/sanctioned events. The provision allowing employees to request an exception was removed.
changes_added: Employees shall not possess or use cigarettes, e-cigarettes, or tobacco products, as defined by Health and Safety Code §161.081 or similar product, on District property or while acting within the scope of their employment on or off school property or at a school-related or school sanctioned event or activity on or off school property.
changes_removed: An employee may request an exception.
timeline_local.notes: Adopted at the December 16, 2025 regular board meeting via two motions (Motion #7301 and Motion #7302), both carried unanimously 6-0. These minutes were approved at the January 20, 2026 meeting. The removal of the exception clause represents a stricter enforcement stance.

timeline_local.summary: This page shows redline changes to policy DH covering employee standards of conduct. Two sections have changes: (1) 'Relationships With Students' expands the notification requirement beyond just 'educator' to include 'employee or person acting as a service provider for the District'; (2) 'Tobacco And E-Cigarettes' adds 'or nicotine products' to the list of prohibited substances and adds a new sentence allowing employees to request an exception for smoking cessation products.
changes_added: employee or person acting as a service provider for the District (replacing/expanding 'educator' in the parent notification requirement)
or nicotine products (added to the list of prohibited substances alongside tobacco and e-cigarettes)
An employee may request an exception from this provision for a smoking cessation product.
changes_removed: educator (replaced by 'employee or person acting as a service provider for the District' in parent notification sentence)
or (changed from 'or e-cigarettes' to include nicotine products — the word 'or' before 'e-cigarettes' appears struck through, restructuring the list)
timeline_local.discussion:
made between what requires policy changes vs. procedural changes
- A draft policy proposal will be brought back for board review at a future meeting

## Board Policy Actions

### Item A: Policy DH (Employee Standards of Conduct) — Nicotine/Tobacco

- Updates the policy to align with Texas Health and Safety Code §161.081 regarding prohibition of tobacco, e-cigarettes, and nicotine products on district property and at school-related events.
- **Amendment:** Struck the sentence allowing employees to "request an exception from this provision for a smoking cessation product," as no such excep

### CQB Local — Technology Resources/Cyber Security
- Requires cyber security training for employees.
- Confirmed the district's cyber security coordinator is in place.

### DH Local — Employee Standards of Conduct
- Updates language regarding vaping, e-cigarettes, and nicotine products to apply to all employees and service providers (not just educators).
- Discussion about whether Zyn pouches (nicotine but no tobacco) would be covered — determined they would be as a "nicotine product."

timeline_local.notes: This is page 254 of the board packet, Policy Update Page Number 20. The document shows a clean (non-redlined) version of policy DH, Version 1, issued 2020-02-05. No tracked changes are visible. It is possible this is the adopted final text rather than a redline/comparison document. The policy addresses: employee accountability to Educators' Code of Ethics, violations of conduct standards, and weapons prohibition with exceptions (Board-authorized firearm use per CKE, locked vehicle storage with Texas handgun license, and District-approved supervised activities per FOD).

description_local: This policy sets the standards of behavior and professional conduct that all Conroe ISD employees are expected to follow in the workplace. It applies to every district employee, from teachers and staff to administrators, and outlines what is considered acceptable and unacceptable conduct on the job. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it helps ensure that the adults working with their children are held to clear, consistent ethical and professional standards.
